exemplar.title:
To what extent does a high ultra-processed food diet affect the cognitive 
development of the foetus in Homo sapiens as measured using early-year cognitive tests?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent does a high ultra-processed food diet affect the cognitive 
development of the foetus in Homo sapiens as measured using early-year cognitive tests?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with comprehensive explanations of ultraprocessed foods, maternal nutrition, and cognitive development.
Research question is clearly stated, focused, and maintained throughout the investigation.
Methodology is justified with a clear rationale for study selection and statistical test choice.
weaknesses: Occasional digressions into historical anecdotes reduce conciseness.
Some sections (e.g., maternal diet) lack direct linkage back to the research question.
Limited discussion of exclusion criteria and statistical assumptions (e.g., normality) in methodology.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates an upperâband handling of Focus and Method. The topic is introduced logically, the research question is explicit and well scoped, and methods are largely appropriate with clear selection rationale. Minor lapses in precision and occasional asides prevent a full 6-point award, but the overall focus and methodological coherence justify a score of 5.

strengths: Terminology (e.g., Î²-coefficients, p-values, neural differentiation) is used accurately and consistently.
Source material is applied effectively, with primary data re-analysed and integrated into tables.
Technical details (e.g., list of additives, placenta nutrient exchange) are presented with coherence.
weaknesses: Much narrative remains descriptive, with limited synthesis across different studies.
A few mechanistic links (e.g., oxidative stress pathway) lack specific citations.
Stylistic inconsistencies (capitalization, symbol formatting) occasionally interrupt flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Knowledge and Understanding is solidly in the upper middle band. The student applies relevant source material coherently and handles biological and statistical concepts with precision. The work stops short of the highest band due to a narrative that is sometimes more descriptive than synthetic and minor citation and formatting lapses. A score of 5 reflects this strong but not exceptional performance.

strengths: Analysis is sound: t-tests are correctly performed and results tied explicitly to the research question.
Evaluation identifies key strengths and weaknesses (e.g., sample size, bias, confounders).
Argument is coherent and well structured from introduction through to conclusion.
weaknesses: Statistical assumptions (normality, variance homogeneity) are not checked or discussed.
No meta-analytic weighting or confidence interval recalculation to deepen quantitative evaluation.
Discussion of practical significance (effect sizes) and mitigation strategies for biases is limited.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Critical Thinking performance sits in the âgoodâ tier but does not reach excellence. The student delivers clear, relevant analysis and evaluation with coherent argumentation, yet omits deeper quantitative checks and advanced synthesis (e.g., meta-analysis, practical significance). A mid-band score of 5 out of 12 accurately reflects these strengths and gaps.

strengths: The report follows the required structure (title page, contents, main sections) with labelled tables and figures.
Word count, title, and page numbers are correctly presented.
Layout is generally neat and navigable.
weaknesses: Abstract and appendices are missing, reducing overall completeness.
Formatting inconsistencies (OCR artifacts in footnotes, misaligned table borders) distract the reader.
Some figures (e.g., fetal development diagram) are not referenced inline, affecting flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is serviceable but falls short of the upper band. While the core structure and basic formatting requirements are met, the absence of an abstract, several formatting errors, and occasional disjointed figure placement justify a score of 2 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The work follows the prescribed EE structure with title page, contents, introduction, methodology, analysis, evaluation, conclusion and bibliography; headings and tables aid navigation.  Abstract and appendices are absent and some figure placement is disjointed, yet the overall structure remains appropriate and easy to follow.

Word count, title and page numbers are present, tables and figures are labelled, and in-text citation format is broadly consistent.  Minor issues exist (inconsistent superscript formatting, table borders misaligned), but layout is generally neat and conforms to academic expectations, justifying the upper band for presentation.

The essay maintains a coherent line of reasoning: introduction â methodology â results â evaluation â conclusion.  Claims generally follow logically from evidence and are reiterated in the conclusion.  Minor redundancies and occasional digressions (history of food processing) create small inconsistencies, but overall the argument is clear and well structured, meriting the middle of the âclear and coherentâ band.

The student introduces ultra-processed foods, maternal nutrition and fetal cognitive development in a logical sequence, defining key ideas (e.g., NOVA classification, neural tube formation) and supporting them with relevant data and figures.  Explanations are generally clear and detailed, showing how each concept links to the research focus.  Minor lapses in precision (occasional repetition, some lengthy anecdotal passages) prevent the treatment from being uniformly concise, but overall the topic is communicated accurately and with breadth, consistent with the upper band.

Source material is largely relevant: primary research papers provide numerical data that are tabulated and re-analysed.  The student applies results to the research question, calculating additional t-values.  However, much of the narrative remains descriptive (restating study aims and sample sizes) and there is limited synthesis between studies.  Application is therefore âmostlyâ but not consistently effective.

The student compares mean cognitive scores, performs t-tests and interprets significance, showing sound analytical technique.  Links to the research question are explicit after each table.  Nevertheless, assumptions underlying the tests are not checked, confidence intervals are not generated for recalculated statistics, and no meta-analytic weighting is attempted, keeping the analysis in the âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ range.

The essay justifies choosing three large cohort studies that share FFQ methodology and comparable cognitive tests, and it outlines use of a two-sample t-test.  This demonstrates some rationale and methodological coherence.  Nevertheless, relying on only three studies limits breadth, and no explanation is offered for excluding other available literature or for assumptions underlying the statistical test (e.g., normality).  Thus the work meets, but does not exceed, the âappropriateâ threshold.

Strengths and weaknesses of the data (sample size, FFQ bias, temporal relevance) are identified and linked to the reliability of the conclusions.  The student recognises confounding factors and ethical constraints, demonstrating evaluation beyond description.  Support for claims is usually drawn from the data, though depth could be greater (e.g., quantitative discussion of effect sizes), placing the work solidly in the âgoodâ band.

The research question is explicitly stated on the title page, is biologically framed, and is narrow enough to be investigated through secondary data analysis of early-year cognitive tests.  The subsequent sections remain focused on this question; all data sets and discussion points are selected to address the extent of cognitive impact.  Some breadth (three different studies, several cognitive domains) keeps it from being laser-focused, but clarity and suitability are fully evident.

Biological and statistical terminologyâe.g., placenta, Î²-coefficient, confidence interval, p-value, neural differentiationâis used accurately and in correct context throughout the essay.  Occasional stylistic slips (capitalisation of foetus, inconsistent symbol formatting) are minor and do not detract from the generally precise and consistent command of concepts.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated, focused and appropriately framed for secondary analysis. This precision strengthens the studyâs direction.
Word count is provided and falls under the 4 000-word limit, meeting IB formatting requirements.
Table of Contents clearly outlines the structure but lacks an abstract. Consider adding a concise abstract to improve navigability.
The title is concise and informative, accurately reflecting the investigation focus on ultra-processed foods and fetal development.
Figure 1 is well labelled and effectively illustrates the NOVA classification framework for processed foods.
The introduction includes useful context but digresses into historical anecdotes. Streamline to maintain focus on maternal nutrition and cognitive outcomes.
Footnotes display OCR artifacts like âlt;sup>â. Ensure consistent citation formatting to enhance professional presentation.
The maternal diet section asserts health issues but lacks direct connection to cognitive outcomes. Tighten discussion around fetal neurodevelopment.
The social and marketing drivers of UPF consumption are described but could be linked more explicitly to maternal dietary patterns.
The list of additives is clearly presented and demonstrates accurate use of technical terminology.
The fetal development diagram is informative but should be explicitly referenced in the surrounding text to guide the reader.
Biological terminology is applied accurately when describing nutrient exchange via the placenta.
The discussion of oxidative stress lacks citation for the mechanistic link. Provide a specific source to strengthen the argument.
The cognitive development section accurately applies developmental concepts and demonstrates coherent knowledge.
Superscript citation formatting is inconsistent (inline math tags). Standardize citation style to improve readability.
Methodology section justifies study selection and explains choice of t-test clearly, showing sound methodological rationale.
Include the t-test formula in text as $$\displaystyle t=\frac{\bar x_1-\bar x_2}{\sqrt{s^2(1/n_1+1/n_2)}}$$ to reinforce understanding rather than relying solely on the figure.
The Shun Lui et al. table of Î²-coefficients with 95 % CI is well integrated and shows advanced data presentation.
Strengths in evaluation correctly identify methodological consistency and large sample sizes, reflecting critical insight.
Weaknesses section is thorough but would be stronger if it suggested mitigation strategies for identified biases.
Puig-VallverdÃº study summary is thorough but would benefit from explaining inclusion/exclusion criteria for chosen cohorts.
Table 1 headers appear misaligned and lack gridlines. Adjust table formatting for clarity and professional presentation.
Interpretation of t-test results is accurate and explicitly tied to p-value thresholds, demonstrating strong analytical skill.
In Cohen et al. results, effect sizes (mean differences) are noted but discussion of practical significance is missing. Consider adding this.
Recommend discussing potential confounding variables (e.g., socioeconomic status) to deepen evaluation of observational data.
The conclusion effectively summarises findings but reiterates earlier content. Streamline for brevity and focus on future implications.
Bibliography entries show inconsistent formatting (duplicate ordering, varying styles). Standardise to a single citation format.
